data.title: Is KLN Logistics Group (SEHK:636) Now Offering Value After Multi Year Share Price Weakness
data.description:
• This article examines whether KLN Logistics Group at HK$6.65 could be a potential bargain or a value trap, and walks through key signals to help you frame that question clearly.
  • The stock has recently been under pressure, with returns of 1.8% over 7 days, 4.9% over 30 days, 7.9% year to date and 0.8% over the past year, alongside declines of 39.0% and 50.4% across 3 and 5 years respectively.
  • Recent attention around KLN Logistics Group has focused less on short term headlines and more on how the share price now compares to various measures of underlying value. This raises an important question for you as an investor: does the current level of sentiment line up with what the fundamentals suggest the stock might be worth?
  • On Simply Wall St's 6 point valuation checklist, KLN Logistics Group currently scores 6 out of 6. The sections that follow walk through the standard valuation approaches before touching on a broader way to think about what that score may mean for you.

Find out why KLN Logistics Group's -0.8% return over the last year is lagging behind its peers.

Advertisement

Approach 1: KLN Logistics Group Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) Analysis

A Discounted Cash Flow model projects the cash a business is expected to generate in the future and then discounts those amounts back to what they could be worth today. It aims to provide an estimate of what the entire stream of future cash flows might justify as a share price at the present time.

For KLN Logistics Group, the model used is a 2 Stage Free Cash Flow to Equity approach, based on cash flow projections in HK$. The latest trailing twelve month free cash flow is about HK$2,470.7m, with Simply Wall St using analyst estimates where available and then extrapolating further years. For example, free cash flow for 2024 is projected at HK$2,379.0m, and the extended projections run through to 2035 with gradually changing free cash flow levels over that period.

When these projected cash flows are discounted back, the model arrives at an estimated intrinsic value of HK$13.51 per share, compared with the current share price of HK$6.65. This indicates a 50.8% difference relative to the DCF estimate, suggesting the shares are priced below this cash flow based valuation.

Result: UNDERVALUED

Approach 2: KLN Logistics Group Price vs Earnings

For profitable companies, the P/E ratio is a useful way to see how much you are paying for each unit of current earnings. It links directly to what the business is already generating today, which many investors treat as a core anchor for value.

What counts as a “normal” or “fair” P/E depends on how the market views a company’s growth outlook and risk profile. Higher expected growth or lower perceived risk can support a higher P/E, while slower expected growth or higher risk can point to a lower P/E being more reasonable.

KLN Logistics Group currently trades on a P/E of 8.24x, compared with the Logistics industry average of 15.06x and a peer average of 33.53x. Simply Wall St also provides a proprietary “Fair Ratio” of 9.04x. This Fair Ratio is designed to be more tailored than simple peer or industry comparisons because it factors in elements such as earnings growth characteristics, profit margins, industry, market cap and key risks.

With the actual P/E of 8.24x sitting below the Fair Ratio of 9.04x by more than 0.10, the shares screen as undervalued on this metric.

Approach 1: CF Industries Holdings Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) Analysis

A Discounted Cash Flow, or DCF, model estimates what a business could be worth today by projecting its future cash flows and then discounting those back to the present.

For CF Industries Holdings, the model used is a 2 Stage Free Cash Flow to Equity approach. The latest twelve month free cash flow is about $2.04b, and analyst inputs plus extrapolated figures point to projected free cash flow of $973m in 2029. Simply Wall St extends analyst estimates beyond the usual 5 year window to build a full 10 year cash flow path.

When all those projected cash flows are discounted back and combined, the model arrives at an estimated intrinsic value of about $95.90 per share. Against the recent share price of around $120, this implies the stock is about 25.3% overvalued on this DCF view.

Result: OVERVALUED

Approach 2: CF Industries Holdings Price vs Earnings

For a profitable company, the P/E ratio is a useful way to think about what you are paying for each dollar of earnings. It links directly to the bottom line and helps you compare CF Industries Holdings with other businesses generating profits today.

What counts as a "normal" P/E depends on what the market expects for future growth and how risky those earnings are perceived to be. Higher expected growth or lower perceived risk can justify a higher P/E, while lower growth or higher risk usually lines up with a lower multiple.

CF Industries Holdings currently trades on a P/E of 12.69x. This sits below both the Chemicals industry average of 26.44x and the peer group average of 27.54x. Simply Wall St also calculates a proprietary “Fair Ratio” of 18.24x, which is the P/E you might expect given factors such as the company’s earnings growth profile, industry, profit margin, market cap and risk characteristics.

This Fair Ratio can be more useful than a simple peer or industry comparison because it adjusts for CF Industries Holdings specific fundamentals rather than assuming all companies deserve similar multiples.
